com.tonic.jaloc.impl.heaps

Class PFloatHeap

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeCollection<A,W>
      • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeList<A,W>
        • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractPrimitiveHeap<PFloatArray,PFloatWriter>
          • com.tonic.jaloc.impl.heaps.PFloatHeap
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public final class PFloatHeap
extends AbstractPrimitiveHeap<PFloatArray,PFloatWriter>
    A growable native float min-heap.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      PFloatHeap()
      Creates an empty heap with zero capacity on the system allocator.
      PFloatHeap(float... values)
      Builds a heap from values in one heapify pass on the system allocator.
      PFloatHeap(long initialCapacity)
      Creates an empty heap with the given starting capacity on the system allocator.
      PFloatHeap(NativeAllocator allocator, float... values)
      Builds a heap from values in one heapify pass on the given allocator.
      PFloatHeap(NativeAllocator allocator, long initialCapacity)
      Creates an empty heap with the given starting capacity on the given allocator.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      float peek()
      Reads the smallest element without removing it.
      float pop()
      Removes and returns the smallest element.
      void push(float value)
      Pushes value, growing if needed.

      • Methods inherited from class com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeList

        clear, ensureCapacity, trimToSize

      • Methods inherited from class com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeCollection

        capacity, close, isEmpty, isOpen, size

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • PFloatHeap

        public PFloatHeap()
        Creates an empty heap with zero capacity on the system allocator.

      • PFloatHeap

        public PFloatHeap(long initialCapacity)
        Creates an empty heap with the given starting capacity on the system allocator.
        Parameters:
        initialCapacity - the starting capacity
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if initialCapacity is negative

      • PFloatHeap

        public PFloatHeap(NativeAllocator allocator,
                  long initialCapacity)
        Creates an empty heap with the given starting capacity on the given allocator.
        Parameters:
        allocator - the allocator to source memory from
        initialCapacity - the starting capacity
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if initialCapacity is negative

      • PFloatHeap

        public PFloatHeap(float... values)
        Builds a heap from values in one heapify pass on the system allocator.
        Parameters:
        values - the values to heapify
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if values is null

      • PFloatHeap

        public PFloatHeap(NativeAllocator allocator,
                  float... values)
        Builds a heap from values in one heapify pass on the given allocator.
        Parameters:
        allocator - the allocator to source memory from
        values - the values to heapify
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if values is null

    • Method Detail

      • push

        public void push(float value)
        Pushes value, growing if needed.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to push
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • pop

        public float pop()
        Removes and returns the smallest element.
        Returns:
        the removed element
        Throws:
        java.util.NoSuchElementException - if empty
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • peek

        public float peek()
        Reads the smallest element without removing it.
        Returns:
        the smallest element
        Throws:
        java.util.NoSuchElementException - if empty
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed
